---
title: "Create map figure"
output: word_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Setup
```{r setup, include= F}
# specify data directory
data_dir <- Sys.getenv("data_dir")

# source setup file
source('setup.R')
```

# Open bibliometric data and identify locations
```{r}
# open data
DF.o <- 
  # open data
  readRDS(file.path(data_dir,
                    "openalex",
                    "btpsych24a_data_bib_processed.Rds"))

# identify country of first author via indexing
DF.o$author1.country <- 
  lapply(c(1 : nrow(DF.o)),
         function(x){
           DF[x, ][['author']][[1]][['institution_country_code']][[1]] %>% 
             return()
           }
  )

# Prepare for merging
DF.o <- DF.o %>% 
  mutate(iso3 = 
           author1.country %>% 
           
           # convert to character and identify NAs
           as.character() %>% 
           if_else(. == "NA",
                   NA,
                   .) %>% 
           
           # convert country code iso3 format (to facilitate later merge)
           countrycode(.,
                       origin = "iso2c", 
                       destination = "iso3c")
         )
```

## Combine with weird indicators
```{r}
# open weird data
DF.w <-
  readRDS(file.path(data_dir,
                    'worlddata',
                    'btpsych24a_weird.Rds')
          )

# for simplicity, calculate average 'ride' scores for each country
DF.w <- DF.w %>% 
  group_by(iso3) %>% 
  summarise(r = mean(r, na.rm = T),
            i = mean(i, na.rm = T),
            d = mean(d, na.rm = T),
            e = mean(e, na.rm = T))

# left join with DF
DF.o.w <- 
  left_join(DF.o,
            DF.w,
            by = "iso3")
```

# Create plots comparing population and sample distribution
Create general plotting function
```{r}
DataViz <- function(outcome,
                    label){
  ggplot(data = DF.w,
         aes(x = .data[[outcome]])) +
  
  # distribution across-countries
  geom_density(color = 'purple4',
               adjust = 2) +
  
  # distribution
  geom_density(data = DF.o.w,
               color = 'blue1',
               adjust = 7) +
  
  xlab(label) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank())
}
```

Create figures
```{r}
r.fig <- DataViz(outcome = 'r',
                 label = 'GDP per capita (millions)')

i.fig <- DataViz(outcome = 'i',
                 label = 'Industrialization')

d.fig <- DataViz(outcome = 'd',
                 label = 'Democratic ratings')

e.fig <- DataViz(outcome = 'e',
                 label = 'Educational attainment')

plot_grid(r.fig,
          i.fig,
          d.fig,
          e.fig)
```

# Create plots comparing distribution across different bands of big team science
Create general plotting function
```{r}
DataViz2 <- function(outcome,
                     label){
  ggplot(data = DF.o.w,
         aes(x = .data[[outcome]])) +
  
  # small team distribution
  geom_density(data = DF.o.w %>% 
                 filter(size.bin == '1 - 4'),
               color = 'green4',
               #adjust = 10
               ) +
    
  # medium team distribution
  geom_density(data = DF.o.w %>% 
                 filter(size.bin == '5 - 9'),
               color = 'yellow4',
               #adjust = 10
               ) +
    
  # big team distribution
  geom_density(data = DF.o.w %>% 
                 filter(authors.n > 9),
               color = 'red4',
               #adjust = 10
               ) +
    
  # actual distribution
  geom_density(data = DF.w,
               linetype = 'dotted',
               #adjust = 2
               ) +
    
  # fix aes
  xlab(label) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank())
}
```

Create figures
```{r}
r.fig <- DataViz2(outcome = 'r',
                  label = 'GDP per capita (millions)')

i.fig <- DataViz2(outcome = 'i',
                  label = 'Industrialization')

d.fig <- DataViz2(outcome = 'd',
                  label = 'Democratic ratings')

e.fig <- DataViz2(outcome = 'e',
                  label = 'Educational attainment')

plot_grid(r.fig,
          i.fig,
          d.fig,
          e.fig)
```

Some analyses
```{r}
DF.o.w <- 
  DF.o.w %>% 
  mutate(group = 
           case_when(size.bin == '1 - 4' ~ 'small',
                     size.bin == '5 - 9' ~ 'medium',
                     authors.n > 9 ~ 'large')
         )

# r
kruskal.test(r ~ group,
             data = DF.o.w)

cor.test(x = DF.o.w$r,
         y = DF.o.w$authors.n,
         alternative = 'two.sided',
         method = 'spearman')

# i
kruskal.test(i ~ group,
             data = DF.o.w)

cor.test(x = DF.o.w$i,
         y = DF.o.w$authors.n,
         alternative = 'two.sided',
         method = 'spearman')

# d
kruskal.test(d ~ group,
             data = DF.o.w)

cor.test(x = DF.o.w$d,
         y = DF.o.w$authors.n,
         alternative = 'two.sided',
         method = 'spearman')
```
